channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Rescue mission launches to save NASA telescope that's falling back to Earth thanks to solar storms - PBS
item.description:
1. Rescue mission launches to save NASA telescope that's falling back to Earth thanks to solar storms  PBS
  2. Daring rescue mission launches to save a 3,200-pound NASA observatory from an untimely end  CNN
  3. Nasa launches mission to save falling space telescope  BBC
  4. NASA launches spacecraft to recover sinking telescope  DW.com
  5. Pegasus launches Swift reboost mission  SpaceNews

item.title: James Webb telescope may have discovered a never-before-seen substance on Pluto and Titan - Live Science
item.description: James Webb telescope may have discovered a never-before-seen substance on Pluto and Titan  Live ScienceSee more headlines & perspectives on Google News

item.title: Astronomers discover Earth’s new ‘next-door neighbor’ — a potentially habitable planet - New York Post
item.description:
1. Astronomers discover Earth’s new ‘next-door neighbor’ — a potentially habitable planet  New York Post
  2. UC Irvine astronomers discover a new Earth-like exoplanet  UC Irvine News
  3. Astronomers discover a potentially habitable planet just 25 light-years away. 'This one is exciting'  Space
  4. Potentially Habitable Super-Earth Found Just 25 Light-Years Away  ScienceAlert
  5. A world just 25 light-years away sits in the narrow zone where liquid water could exist on its surface — and astronomers at UC Irvine have now revised its mass down to just 2.3 times Earth’s, making it look less like a gas-shrouded mini-Neptune and more like s  Space Daily

item.title: 'It's more than a hope, it's a guarantee': The Rubin Observatory's 10-year movie of the universe is about to 'blow our minds,' chief scientist says - Live Science
item.description:
1. 'It's more than a hope, it's a guarantee': The Rubin Observatory's 10-year movie of the universe is about to 'blow our minds,' chief scientist says  Live Science
  2. A giant telescope goes on a decade-long search for dark matter  The Washington Post
  3. A Dazzling Census of Our Cosmos Begins  The New York Times
  4. Rubin Observatory begins 10-year sky survey with stunning image  Mashable
  5. Vera Rubin’s search for dark matter  The Economist

item.title: 'Milestone': Scientists claim to build synthetic cell, raising concerns in step toward artificial life - Fox News
item.description:
1. 'Milestone': Scientists claim to build synthetic cell, raising concerns in step toward artificial life  Fox News
  2. World's first synthetic cell that can grow, divide and replicate created at U of M  FOX 9 Minneapolis-St. Paul
  3. Scientists React to the Lab-Made, Yet Lifelike, SpudCell  The New York Times
  4. Scientists Say They've Made Cells That Feed, Grow and Reproduce, Bringing Them One Step Closer to Building Life From Scratch  Smithsonian Magazine
  5. Scientists have built a cell in a lab from the ground up  The Economist

item.title: Hobbit-like human relatives may have been on a less advanced evolutionary path - CNN
item.description:
1. Hobbit-like human relatives may have been on a less advanced evolutionary path  CNN
  2. Ancient ‘hobbits’ feasted on Komodo dragons’ leftovers  Scientific American
  3. ‘Hobbit’ hominins scavenged meat left over by Komodo dragons  New Scientist
  4. New Theory of Smallest Human: Not a Hunter, But Eater of Lizard Leftovers  Haaretz
  5. Our ancient ‘hobbit’ cousins ate dragon leftovers  National Geographic

item.title: NASA's Chandra telescope reveals Milky Way's outer reaches may stretch farther than previously known - Fox News
item.description:
1. NASA's Chandra telescope reveals Milky Way's outer reaches may stretch farther than previously known  Fox News
  2. NASA’s Chandra Examines Milky Way at Arms’ Length  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. XMM-Newton and Chandra help revise distance to Milky Way's outer spiral arms  Phys.org
  4. The Milky Way’s arms might not look as we thought  EarthSky
  5. XMM-Newton gamma-ray detection shows the Milky Way is bigger than previously thought  The Brighter Side of News

item.title: ‘Strong’ Northern Lights Alert: 26 States May See Aurora On July 4 - Forbes
item.description:
1. ‘Strong’ Northern Lights Alert: 26 States May See Aurora On July 4  Forbes
  2. Sun fires off 10 solar flares in 24 hours as multiple Earth-bound CMEs raise northern lights hopes for July 4 weekend  Space
  3. Northern Lights could dazzle the sky in these states due to solar storm ahead of Fourth of July  FOX Weather
  4. Northern lights could dazzle in Oregon and Washington on July 4 weekend  KOIN.com
  5. 'Machine-gun sun' could bring auroras to more than a dozen states July 4th weekend  Live Science

item.title: A Chinese Spacecraft Just Arrived At Earth’s ‘Quasi Moon’ - Forbes
item.description:
1. A Chinese Spacecraft Just Arrived At Earth’s ‘Quasi Moon’  Forbes
  2. China’s Tianwen-2 mission has (probably) arrived at a quasi-moon of Earth  The Planetary Society
  3. A tiny “quasi-moon” called Kamoʻoalewa shadows Earth’s orbit and may be a piece of the actual Moon blasted into space by an ancient impact — and China’s Tianwen-2 spacecraft is now closing in to bring a sample back to Earth.  Space Daily
  4. China's Tianwen-2 Probe Has Likely Reached Earth's "Quasi-moon" Kamo‘oalewa. Now The Fun Begins  IFLScience
  5. “Tianwen-2” Lands on Kamoʻoalewa. What Is This Mission and What Is Its Purpose?  Universe Space Tech

item.title: Physicists Simulated a Black Hole in a Lab. Then It Started to 'Evaporate'. - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Physicists Simulated a Black Hole in a Lab. Then It Started to 'Evaporate'.  ScienceAlert
  2. Backreaction of stimulated Hawking radiation in an optical analogue  Nature
  3. Hawking radiation breakthrough offers new insight into black hole physics  Innovation News Network
  4. Scientists Make Major Black Hole Discovery That Could Explain Stephen Hawking's Greatest Mystery  NDTV
  5. Backreaction of Stimulated Hawking Radiation Revealed  Bioengineer.org

item.title: 'Stellar death is not the end': James Webb Space Telescope glimpses the fate of the solar system in a weird exoplanet orbiting a dead star - Space
item.description:
1. 'Stellar death is not the end': James Webb Space Telescope glimpses the fate of the solar system in a weird exoplanet orbiting a dead star  Space
  2. ‘Bizarre’ planet orbiting dead star may preview fate of our solar system  CNN
  3. NASA’s Webb Studies How Planet Survived Death of its Star  NASA Science (.gov)
  4. The Earth May Survive Being Engulfed By The Sun, But Life Will Still End In 1.8 Billion Years Anyway  IFLScience
  5. Aerosols and hydrocarbons in the atmosphere of a white dwarf planet  Nature

item.title: Titan, Saturn’s Largest Moon, Could Become a ‘Gas Station’ for Deep Space Missions - ZME Science
item.description:
1. Titan, Saturn’s Largest Moon, Could Become a ‘Gas Station’ for Deep Space Missions  ZME Science
  2. Could humans someday explore Saturn's moon Titan, or will humanoid robots do it for us?  Space
  3. Methane, natural fuel lakes, ice, and a nitrogen-rich atmosphere make Titan central to NASA's plans for the first "space fuel station" to speed up missions beyond Mars.  CPG Click Oil and Gas
  4. Titan’s Resources and their Utilization  Astrobiology Web
  5. ⛽ Titan, the interplanetary gas station  Techno-Science.net

item.description:

item.title: Why Antarctica froze millions of years before the Arctic – new research - The Conversation
item.description:
1. Why Antarctica froze millions of years before the Arctic – new research  The Conversation
  2. Scientists uncover why Antarctica became engulfed by ice millions of years before the Arctic  Phys.org
  3. Here's why the South Pole froze over before the North Pole  Reuters
  4. The Breakup of Gondwana Over 100 Million Years Ago May Be Why Antarctica Has Ice Today  eos.org
  5. Cracking the Ice: Unveiling the Mysteries of Antarctic Glaciation  Devdiscourse

item.title: In April 2025, four private astronauts on SpaceX’s Fram2 mission became the first humans ever to circle Earth in a true polar orbit, flying directly over both poles — a route no government crewed space program had taken in more than six decades of human sp - Space Daily
item.description: In April 2025, four private astronauts on SpaceX’s Fram2 mission became the first humans ever to circle Earth in a true polar orbit, flying directly over both poles — a route no government crewed space program had taken in more than six decades of human sp  Space Daily

item.title: A martian rock has lots of carbon on it, and it's not clear why - Ars Technica
item.description:
1. A martian rock has lots of carbon on it, and it's not clear why  Ars Technica
  2. Ancient Martian River Channel Yields Complex Organics  Universe Today
  3. NASA’s Curiosity rover has identified the most diverse set of organic molecules yet found on Mars, including seven never before detected there, from a sample drilled in 2020 and studied for years before the chemistry finally came into focus  Space Daily
  4. NASA rover detects potential signs of ancient life on Mars  The Hindu
  5. 15 million years of laughter, organics on Mars, hope for defeating Parkinson's  realnoevremya.com

item.title: Sea level doesn’t rise at the same rate everywhere – we mapped where Antarctica’s ice melt would have the biggest impact - Yahoo News UK

item.title: Mountain lions moved into a tiny Californian preserve. What happened next astounded scientists - BBC Wildlife Magazine
item.description:
1. Mountain lions moved into a tiny Californian preserve. What happened next astounded scientists  BBC Wildlife Magazine
  2. One mountain lion changed the food web in a California suburb, study finds  news - Mongabay
  3. When mountain lions began appearing more often on trail cameras at a small California nature preserve, coyotes and deer shifted away from nighttime activity, and the woody plants those deer used to eat increased sixty four fold  Space Daily
  4. Mountain lions changed everything in this tiny California preserve  ScienceDaily
  5. Mountain lions left this suburb a century ago; one returned to Stanford's preserve in 2015, and woody plan  The Economic Times

item.title: Over six days in July of 1994, 21 fragments of a comet named Shoemaker-Levy 9 crashed one after another into the atmosphere of Jupiter as telescopes across the world watched — the first time in recorded history that human beings had ever directly observe - Space Daily

  4. Kyushu University Team Finds Link Between Gravity And Particle States  Quantum Zeitgeist

item.title: In November, a spacecraft that has spent eight years falling toward the Sun will finally settle into orbit around Mercury, a planet we have seen up close fewer times than almost any other in the solar system. - Space Daily
item.description: In November, a spacecraft that has spent eight years falling toward the Sun will finally settle into orbit around Mercury, a planet we have seen up close fewer times than almost any other in the solar system.  Space Daily

item.title: A rare meteorite found in the Sahara may be a surviving shard of a lost Moon-sized world — one that formed beside the young Sun, followed a path unlike Earth or Mars, and was smashed apart while the planets were still being built. - Space Daily
item.description: A rare meteorite found in the Sahara may be a surviving shard of a lost Moon-sized world — one that formed beside the young Sun, followed a path unlike Earth or Mars, and was smashed apart while the planets were still being built.  Space Daily

item.description:
